name: Mel Powell
abstract: Mel Powell (born Melvin Epstein) (February 12, 1923 – April 24, 1998) was an American Pulitzer Prize-winning composer, and the founding dean of the music department at the California Institute of the Arts. He served as a music educator for over 40 years, first at Mannes College of Music and Queens College, then Yale University, and final…

concerts.label: All-Star Jazz Concert and Recording Session

works_created.t: Settings for Clarinet, Violin, and Piano: Madrigal
Trio for Violin, Cello, and Piano, Op. 5

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Strazzeri
about_quotes.evidence: I grew up listening to my Uncle Joe playing swing style piano, like Teddy Wilson, Art Tatum, Mel Powell kind of a bag.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Strazzeri interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: sometimes we used to do a Good Morning Blues program which was early in the morning, that consist of Mel Powell piano... and me

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: Mel Powell, the piano player with Benny Goodman, used to bring him groceries to keep him alive.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Milt Hinton
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Milt Hinton) does not speak about Mel Powell. The mention of Mel Powell occurs only in the interviewer's turn: 'Did you hear that marvelous story that Mel tells about him, Mel Powell?'
about_quotes.doc_title: Milt Hinton interviewed by Joe Williams, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Sol Yaged
about_quotes.evidence: Mel Powell was the pianist at that time. ... I was there the night they introduced Peggy Lee to sing a song called “Why Don’t You Do Right?” ... It was the Hotel New Yorker on 34th Street.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sol Yaged interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: Mel Powell was on one of the cruises. And Mel Powell worked with Benny Goodman way before I did. And we sat there one night talking about Benny Goodman stories
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 5, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]
